facts.text: Bradfordville began as a settlement between 1829 and 1832 when the Bradford brothers moved from North Carolina to farm large tracts of land.
Thomas Anderson Bradford, born February 13, 1790, founded Walnut Hill Plantation on the area that became Bradfordville.
Edward Bradford, born August 2, 1798, established Pine Hill Plantation, a 3,270‑acre cotton plantation that once dominated the region.
The most successful Bradford brother later founded Horseshoe Plantation east‑southeast of Lake Iamonia, adding another large estate to the county.
Bradfordville sits in Florida’s Red Hills Region, where rolling hills, ravines, lakes and ponds create a distinctive landscape.
Lake Iamonia, one of Florida’s larger natural lakes, lies northwest of Bradfordville and is a popular spot for fishing and boating.
